problem.title: Summary Ranges
problem.description: You are given a sorted unique integer array nums.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Linear Scan
solutions.description: A single pass tracks the start of the current consecutive run; whenever the next element is not exactly one greater than the current end (or we reach the array's end), the completed range is emitted and a new range begins. This works because the input is sorted with no duplicates, so a gap greater than 1 unambiguously marks a range boundary. The key insight is maintaining only two pointers — start and end of the current run — rather than storing all elements.
